items.title: 'People assume we're not real': MPs open up about mental health challenges

children.content: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

articles.title: TassieCast: Do we care enough about our pollies' mental health?

children.content: In what's believed to be the biggest study of its kind, a staggering 70 per cent of almost 300 of our federal, state and local politicians who took part say the job has adversely affected their mental health.

image.altText: Man sitting in garden looking stressed

gallery.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

items.title: Study finds decades of 'intensive' logging worsens bushfire risk

children.content: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

image.altText: Forest lit up by unseen bushfire flames.

items.title: Call for 'collective action' to address gendered violence in Tasmania

children.content: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

image.altText: Sally Haynes and Carolyn Self launching "Justice for Her" 2026-04-08 10:04:00

items.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

articles.title: The emperor penguin and Antarctic fur seal are now considered endangered

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

items.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses

children.content: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

image.altText: A man in a 'PROTECT TAKAYNA' shirt stands in front of a bus going to Devonport.

items.title: Grace Tame Foundation announces closure

children.content: The Grace Tame Foundation, established by the Tasmanian-born survivor of child sexual abuse, has announced it is closing because securing funding is becoming "increasingly challenging".

image.altText: A woman wearing a grey cardigan with her hair in a pony tail looks seriously at the camera in a building corridor.

items.title: Two leaders, two very different reactions to Trump's Iran grandstanding

children.content: If you want a basic measure of the difference between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, compare their reactions to US President Donald Trump's extraordinary threat this week that "a whole civilisation will die tonight".

image.altText: Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan

items.title: Super members blindsided by change in fund terms and conditions

children.content: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

gallery.altText: Lauren sits on the couch with laptop in the lap, her hair in a high bun

thumbnail.altText: Lauren sits on the couch with laptop in the lap, her hair in a high bun

items.title: 'Never friends': Melania Trump criticises Epstein 'lies' in unusual address

children.content: In an unusual White House address, Melania Trump denies having a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and complains about "mean-spirited" attempts to smear her.

image.altText: Melania Trump

items.title: Construction businesses vulnerable to supply chain shock, experts warn

children.content: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

image.altText: Housing construction

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans

children.content: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

gallery.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

items.title: Travelling to Europe? New biometric border rules start today

children.content: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.

image.altText: A man operates an automated EES kiosk at a european border

items.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk — it's close to collapse

children.content: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

avatar.alt: A vision impaired woman with long hair in a blue dress holding a cane

items.title: Australian role in Hormuz depends on permanent ceasefire: Marles

children.content: Australia has been involved in talks about assisting the re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz but any action can only take place if a ceasefire becomes permanent, Richard Marles says. Follow live.

image.altText: Close up of Richard Marles wearing a suit and tie.

items.title: Queensland state MP Jimmy Sullivan found dead

children.content: MP for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan has been found dead in Brisbane.

image.altText: Jimmy Sullivan speaks in the state parliament.

children.content: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

items.title: Sister pushes to raise awareness of male eating disorders after tragic loss

children.content: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men at are risk.

items.title: Animals on wildlife market more likely to carry human-infecting disease, report finds

children.content: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

image.altText: four birds sit on a ledge inside a market

items.title: Wall Street, oil prices up as investors consider Middle East peace plan

children.content: A relief rally has continued on Wall Street overnight as investors mull the two-week ceasefire deal in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock market watcher stands in front of an ASX board.

items.title: Vladimir Putin declares 32-hour ceasefire in Ukraine for Orthodox Easter weekend

children.content: The Russian president declared the ceasefire following Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposing one earlier this week.

image.altText: A man flicking a brush with water onto a man in a military uniform standing beside the boot of a car.

items.title: From Apollo to Artemis, the lunar legacy of the Parkes 'Dish' continues

children.content: The Parkes Observatory — also known as The Dish — continues a legacy of supporting lunar missions that started more than 50 years ago, helping Artemis II go where humanity has not since 1972.

image.altText: The moons surface with earth in the background

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

gallery.altText: A young male athelete smiles while holding a large golden trophy.

thumbnail.altText: A young male athelete smiles while holding a large golden trophy.

items.title: Truck driver charged over Sunshine Coast crash that killed 3yo

children.content: A truck driver has been charged with drug driving over a crash that killed a three-year-old girl and critically injured her one-year-old sister.

image.altText: Three-year-old Ineza King dies following Banya truck crash

items.title: Mark Gregory's mum just wants to know what happened to her son 35 years ago

children.content: Mark Gregory was just 28 when he was killed by a hit-and-run driver while walking home from a party in 1991. His family just wants answers.

image.altText: three people sitting on a couch

items.title: Farmers take action as dry grips Queensland region

children.content: Queensland farmers gripped by a dry spell are turning to drastic and inventive measures: offloading most of their livestock and laying kilometres of water pipelines.

image.altText: A farmer and a dog beside a low white water tank.

items.title: Police not ruling out foul play as search continues for missing NSW woman

children.content: The daughter of Yvonne Whear, 73, who went missing in February, says there is no evidence to suggest her mum left her northern NSW home of her own accord.

items.title: Herb in cold and flu products linked to life-threatening anaphylaxis

children.content: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

items.title: Victoria's 2026 election to host Australia's largest trial of low-sensory voting

children.content: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.
